Title: Metabolic Engineering of Plant-derived (E)-β-farnesene Synthase Genes for a Novel Type of Aphid-resistant Genetically Modified Crop Plants

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase family; sesquiterpene synthase; conserved DDxxD motif; conserved Arg/His residues; J-K loop
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts farnesyl diphosphate to the acyclic sesquiterpene (E)-β-farnesene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Aphid alarm pheromone; repels aphids and attracts natural enemies used as a foraging cue
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression in tobacco caused emission of pure (E)-β-farnesene, repelled peach aphids, and attracted predatory lacewings, helping minimize aphid infestation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaβFS1 from Artemisia annua encodes a plant (E)-β-farnesene synthase that converts FPP to (E)-β-farnesene. 2. Overexpression in tobacco resulted in emission of pure (E)-β-farnesene. 3. The transgenic tobacco repelled peach aphids and significantly attracted lacewings, indicating utility for aphid control.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; recombinant enzyme characterization; transgenic overexpression in tobacco; GC-MS volatile profiling; aphid and lacewing behavioral assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Emission level was lower than expected (approximately 1.55 to 4.65 ng/d per g fresh weight in transgenic tobacco); repellence to aphids was weaker than expected, probably due to other tobacco volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising candidate gene for engineering aphid-resistant crops that emit pure (E)-β-farnesene and recruit natural enemies; may require additional engineering to increase sesquiterpene flux/emission
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summarizes prior work; conserved catalytic features include DDxxD motif and residues Arg290, Arg292, Asp551 and Lys561 in AaβFS1.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase family; sesquiterpene synthase; conserved DDxxD motif; conserved Arg/His residues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Aphid alarm pheromone; repels aphids and attracts natural enemies
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression in tobacco caused emission of pure (E)-β-farnesene, repelled peach aphids, and attracted predatory lacewings
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaβFS2 from Artemisia annua is a plant (E)-β-farnesene synthase. 2. Tobacco overexpressing AaβFS2 emitted pure (E)-β-farnesene. 3. The engineered plants repelled aphids and attracted lacewings, supporting its value for aphid-resistant crop engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; transgenic overexpression in tobacco; GC-MS volatile profiling; aphid and lacewing behavioral assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low sesquiterpene emission level; repellence to aphids not as strong as expected, likely influenced by other tobacco volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for metabolic engineering of aphid-resistant crops; optimization of precursor supply and emission may be needed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper; discussed together with AaβFS1 as sweet wormwood-derived genes with practical engineering potential.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Aphid alarm pheromone; repels aphids and attracts parasitoid natural enemies
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Engineering Arabidopsis with peppermint EβF synthase caused emission of extremely pure (E)-β-farnesene, eliciting alarm/repellent responses in Myzus persicae and arrestant responses in Diaeretiella rapae
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A peppermint-derived (E)-β-farnesene synthase enabled Arabidopsis to emit extremely pure (E)-β-farnesene. 2. The volatile altered insect behavior at two trophic levels, repelling aphids and attracting a parasitoid. 3. This established proof-of-concept for engineering insect-pheromone-producing plants for aphid resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression in Arabidopsis; volatile analysis; aphid behavioral assays; parasitoid behavioral assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Behavioral assays with Myzus persicae and Diaeretiella rapae
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong proof-of-concept gene for engineering crops that emit insect alarm pheromone for aphid management
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review does not provide the specific gene symbol/accession in this excerpt.
